title: Lines Upon A Diamond Cross, Worn On Her Bosom By Miss C.M.
content:
Well on that neck, sweet Kitty! may you wear
    The sparkling cross, with hopes to soften Heaven;
    For trust me, tho' so very young and fair,
    Thou hast some little sins to be forgiven: - 
    For all the hopes which wit and grace can spread,
    For all the sighs which countless charms can move,
    Fall, lovely Kitty! on thy youthful head;
    Yet fall they gently - for the crime is love.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Carr (Sir), titled "Lines Upon A Diamond Cross, Worn On Her Bosom By Miss C.M.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
